prompt:
You are an expert industrial quality control analyst.
I will provide you with descriptions of 5 NORMAL (defect-free) object samples.

Your task is to identify the strict INVARIANTS — the characteristics that are completely identical and required across ALL normal samples.

[Descriptions of Normal Samples]
[ Normal object Description 1 ]
1. Components: 
   - Two tangerines
   - One nectarine (peach)
   - A portion of granola or cereal mix on the right side, containing banana chips and almonds

2. Quantities:
   - Exactly two tangerines
   - Exactly one nectarine
   
3. Spatial Arrangement:
   - The two tangerines are located in a specific section at the left-hand side.
   - The single nectarine is also positioned to the left but slightly above the first row of tangerines.

4. Visual Appearance:
   - The breakfast box has white color with distinct sections for different food items.
   - The size appears rectangular enough to accommodate all components comfortably without overlapping them excessively.


< Specific Constraints for this Breakfast Box >
- On the far-left compartment there must be exactly three fruits arranged vertically from top to bottom; these include an apple, followed by two orange-colored citrus fruit which appear as tangerines due to their smaller size compared to standard oranges.
- To the right of those fruits lies another larger compartment filled predominantly with dry cereal grains that have been baked until lightly browned giving off a golden hue indicative of being oatmeal-based mixed with other grain varieties such as wheat berries etc., along with some sliced bananas scattered throughout adding natural sweetness & texture contrast against crunchy cereals while providing additional fiber content too!

[ Normal object Description 2 ]
1. Components: 
   - Two tangerines
   - One nectarine (apple)
   - A portion of granola or cereal mix on the right side containing banana chips and almonds

2. Quantities:
   - Exactly two tangerines
   - Exactly one nectarine
   
3. Spatial Arrangement:
   - The two tangerines are located in a row, with each tangerine placed next to another.
   - The nectarine is positioned above these two tangerines.

4. Visual Appearance:
   - The left-hand section contains fruits that have an orange hue due to their coloration as tangerines; they should be brightly colored.
   - The apple has red skin mixed with some yellowish areas near its stem area indicating it's ripe but not overly so.
   - On the right-hand side, there’s a mixture consisting mainly of light brown granola pieces along with slices of bananas which appear slightly darker than the granola itself because of oxidation from being cut open earlier during preparation for this meal combination.


< Normal Constraints for a breakfast_box >
- The breakfast box always contains exactly three distinct sections divided by partitions within the container.
  - Section 1: Left-side compartment holding fruit items including two whole tangerines stacked vertically atop each other followed immediately below them horizontally aligned pair-wise arrangement of additional single tangerine alongside previously mentioned ones together forming triangular pyramid shape when viewed head-on perspective looking directly down into said space filled up entirely without any gaps between objects present therein at all times regardless what else might occupy remaining portions elsewhere inside same overall structure comprised solely out-of-viewed components such as those found occupying central region adjacent borderline edges where walls meet bottom surface level line demarcating boundary separating different zones based upon functionally assigned roles pertaining specifically towards consumption patterns observed throughout history among various cultures worldwide over centuries past until now continuing forward indefinitely future generations onwards too!

[ Normal object Description 3 ]
1. Components: 
   - Two tangerines
   - One nectarine

2. Quantities:
   - Exactly two tangerines
   - Exactly one nectarine
   
3. Spatial Arrangement:
   - The two tangerines and the nectarine are located on the left-hand side of the box.
   
4. Visual Appearance:
   - Tangerines should be orange in color with a smooth, slightly textured surface showing natural segmentation patterns at their base where they attach to stems or calyxes (if present).
   - Nectarine must have reddish-yellow skin transitioning into white flesh underneath when cut open.

5. Right-hand Side Composition:
   - A generous portion of granola occupying most of this section; it is light brown with visible oats and possibly other grains mixed together.
   - Adjacent to the granola but separated by its texture contrast lies an equal mix of banana chips and almonds. Banana chips appear yellow-brown while almonds show off their characteristic dark brown hue and rough outer shell textures.


The breakfast_box adheres strictly to these components, quantities, spatial arrangement rules, and visual appearance guidelines ensuring that each element fits precisely within designated areas for optimal aesthetic appeal as well as functional organization during consumption time.

[ Normal object Description 4 ]
1. Components: 
   - Two tangerines
   - One nectarine

2. Quantities:
   - Exactly two tangerines
   - Exactly one nectarine
   
3. Spatial Arrangement:
   - The two tangerines and the nectarine are always located on the left-hand side of the box.
   
4. Visual Appearance:
   - Tangerines should be bright orange with a green stem visible at their top ends, positioned vertically or horizontally but not overlapping each other nor the nectarine.

5. Right-hand Side Composition (fixed ratio):
   - A substantial amount of granola filling most of this section from corner to corner without any gaps in between.
   - Below the granola is an equal mix of banana chips and almonds occupying roughly half of that space below it.


The breakfast_box must contain exactly these items as described above for proper adherence to its constraints.

[ Normal object Description 5 ]
1. Components: 
   - Two tangerines
   - One nectarine (apple)
   - A portion of granola or cereal mix on the right side, containing almonds and banana chips

2. Quantities:
   - Exactly two tangerines.
   - Exactly one nectarine.

3. Spatial Arrangement:
   - The two tangerines are located in a row at the left-hand side near each other but not touching.
   - The single nectarine is placed above these two tangerines to form an L-shape with them together forming part of the left section of the box's interior space.
   - On the right-hand side within another compartment separate from where fruits reside, there should be a mixture comprising granola/cereal pieces along with sliced bananas and whole almonds arranged neatly without overlapping excessively; this area occupies roughly half of that particular segment compared to fruit-filled zone which takes up remaining smaller fractional share for balance overall composition inside container.

4. Visual Appearance:
   - Tangerines have bright orange coloration while their peel texture appears slightly rougher than smooth surface seen upon adjacent apple’s skin tone exhibiting reddish-yellow gradient hues transitioning into deeper red shades towards stem end region.
   - Nectarine displays uniform light pink flesh interspersed sporadically by tiny white speckles indicating presence of seeds beneath its translucent outer layer revealing subtle hints about inner structure through semi-opaque membrane covering it entirely except around central core cavity exposed when cut open longitudinally down middle line dividing both halves evenly apart allowing easy access during consumption process later on if desired.
   - Granola/Cereal blend consists mainly brown grains mixed with occasional flecks of greenery possibly representing oats or wheat kernels alongside scattered bits of dried fruit like raisins or cranberries adding sweetness contrast against nutty undertones provided by roasted nuts such as pecans walnuts hazelnuts etc.; all combined create visually appealing palette dominated predominantly by earthy tones complemented occasionally by pops of vibrant colors coming from various ingredients used throughout production cycle resulting in final product ready-to-eat snack option ideal for breakfast time!

[Normality Constraints Provided by Engineer]
- The breakfast box always contains exactly two tangerines and one nectarine that are always located on the left-hand side of the box.
- The ratio and relative position of the cereals and the mix of banana chips and almonds on the right-hand side are fixed.

Analyze the descriptions and extract the core logical rules.
Format your output exactly like this:
1. Components: [List the exact objects that must be present]
2. Quantities: [Exact numbers required, e.g., "exactly two washers"]
3. Spatial Arrangement: [Where items must be located, e.g., "only on the left side"]
4. Visual Appearance: [Specific colors, shapes, or orientations required]

Keep it concise, factual, and strictly based on the provided descriptions.
response:
1. Components: [Two tangerines, one nectarine (peach), a portion of granola or cereal mix containing banana chips and almonds]
2. Quantities: [Exactly two tangerines; exactly one nectarine]
3. Spatial Arrangement: [The two tangerines are located in the left-hand side section at the bottom row with each next to another; The single nectarine is positioned above these two tangerines forming an L-shape together with them on top part of said area]
4. Visual Appearance: [Tangerines should be orange-colored having smooth slightly textured surface showing natural segmentation patterns where they attach stems/calyxes if present; Nectarine must have reddish-yellow skin transitioning into white flesh underneath when cut open]
